title: hieracium alpinum subsp. piliferum
intro: Hieracium alpinum subsp. piliferum, also known as the alpine hawkweed or mountain hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and it has been found to have medicinal properties that include acting as a diuretic and a laxative. The health benefits of Hieracium alpinum subsp. piliferum include reducing fever, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its safety and efficacy as a medicinal herb have not been extensively scientifically studied.
